hero.title: warasugi
hero.subtitle: CLI-focused engineer | Paper plugins · Discord bots · Android | Homelab

about.title: About
about.description: Information systems student based in Hokkaido. Focused on lightweight, high-performance systems and CLI tools.
about.hobby: Lately I've been learning by playing with my self-hosted server.

infrastructure.title: Self-Hosted Infrastructure
infrastructure.notes1: Network core — OPNsense (HP-2 VM500) separates and routes 192.168.0.x / 192.168.1.x
infrastructure.notes2: DNS redundancy — AdGuard Home deployed per segment (1.x: CT106 / 0.x: CT107)
infrastructure.notes3: Zero-trust redundancy — Twingate deployed across 2 segments (1.x: HP-2 CT105 / 0.x: Dell CT102)
infrastructure.notes4: Public exposure via Cloudflare Tunnel only — portfolio.warasugi.com (Dell CT103) served with zero inbound
infrastructure.notes5: Minecraft runs on a dedicated bare-metal box (Debian 13 · under 0.x · repurposed Kasm WS) hosting an always-on Purpur survival server — access only via off-site (Tokyo) Velocity with zero direct exposure (linked to Dell CT301). GraalVM 17/21/25.1 in use; webmap exposed via Cloudflare Tunnel
infrastructure.notes6: World backup — DriveBackupV2 transfers to HP-2 MC-Backup (CT100) at 4 AM (40MB/s bandwidth cap · 1-week retention). Retrieval via FileBrowser (exposed over Cloudflare Tunnel)
infrastructure.notes7: Monitoring & defense-in-depth — Zabbix (Dell CT400) + Ed25519 SSH keys + TOTP 2FA + Anubis
infrastructure.dgmAria: waras-nw 3-node Proxmox VE cluster diagram (+ bare-metal Minecraft server)
infrastructure.dgmTitle: Home-lab infrastructure — waras-nw 3-node Proxmox VE cluster
infrastructure.dgmSecurity2: • Ed25519 SSH keys / no password auth
infrastructure.dgmLegend1: waras-nw — Proxmox VE 9.x, 3 nodes (HP-1 / HP-2 / Dell)
infrastructure.dgmLegend3: Zero-trust — Twingate redundant per segment (1.x: CT105 / 0.x: CT102)
infrastructure.dgmLegend4: DNS filtering — AdGuard Home redundant per segment (1.x: CT106 / 0.x: CT107)
infrastructure.dgmLegend5: Public — portfolio.warasugi.com served via Cloudflare Tunnel with zero inbound / no external ports
infrastructure.dgmLegend7: Bare-metal MC — always-on Purpur survival (i7-3770 / 16GB / SSD 256GB · Debian 13 · repurposed Kasm WS), outside cluster, under 0.x
infrastructure.dgmLegend8: World backup — DriveBackupV2 → HP-2 MC-Backup (CT100). 4 AM · 40MB/s cap · 1-week retention
infrastructure.netAria: Traffic-path diagram: OPNsense segmentation and zero-trust
infrastructure.netMcVia: ← Via Tokyo Velocity (linked to Dell CT301)
infrastructure.netSeg0Row3: 🌐 portfolio [CT103 · Dell] — ← exposed via Cloudflare Tunnel
infrastructure.netSeg0Row5: 🎮 Minecraft bare-metal — via Tokyo Velocity / webmap via CF Tunnel
infrastructure.netSecAnnot1: 🔒 Zero external ports — no inbound (Cloudflare Tunnel) / Minecraft also via Tokyo Velocity
infrastructure.netSecAnnot2: 🔐 SSH · Proxmox WebUI · internal services only via Twingate zero-trust (2-segment redundancy)
infrastructure.netLeg1: ① Public route — Internet → Cloudflare Tunnel → portfolio.warasugi.com (zero inbound)
infrastructure.netLeg2: ② Management route — Twingate zero-trust → SSH / Proxmox / internal services
infrastructure.netLeg3: ③ Minecraft (bare-metal · under 0.x) — exposed via off-site (Tokyo) Velocity (zero inbound at home)
